requirements.title: Fix trigger configuration to use preemptiveWatermark instead of hardcoded 0.6
Fix Level 4 budget enforcement in removeOldCompressibleMessages
acceptance: Running token counter updates as candidates added

requirements.title: Fix compressMessage to preserve code block structure
acceptance: No regex-based wholesale code block replacement

constraints.title: Circuit breaker + lossy truncation safety net

terminology.definition: 4-level progressive content stripping: reasoning → bulk output → artifacts → full message removal
3-tier dispatch: NONE (<60%), MICRO (60-90%, no LLM), FULL (90%+, structured summarization)
3-failure threshold → lossy truncation (system + last 10 msgs) → 5-turn cooldown
Previous summary kept immutable as prefix, new content appended — preserves prompt cache
Projects next turn token usage (currentTokens + outputHeadroom) and triggers before user's next turn

non_goals.title: Semantic importance scoring (LLM/embeddings)
non_goals.rationale: Requires additional dependencies and latency; regex-only is an acknowledged tradeoff

non_goals.title: Tree-sitter AST parsing for code blocks
non_goals.rationale: Requires dependency and complexity increase; deferred to future version

non_goals.title: Dynamic file inclusion (Cursor-style semantic search)
non_goals.rationale: Out of scope for a compaction extension; different problem domain

non_goals.title: Query-conditioned compression
non_goals.rationale: Requires prediction model; deferred to future version

non_goals.title: LRU Token estimation cache
non_goals.rationale: Need to verify performance bottleneck first; deferred to optimization phase

insights.summary: 7 unique capabilities unmatched by any alternative: 4-level graduated eviction, circuit breaker, preemptive trigger, structured summary, no-LLM default, content-aware estimation, cross-provider awareness
3 Critical bugs: trigger config dead (shouldCompact 0.6 + hardWatermark 0.5), Level 4 budget not enforced (initialTokens frozen), code block destruction (compressMessage 50-char stub)
4 advertised features do not exist: multi-pass summarization, quality scoring, LRU cache, entropy extraction — severe documentation/implementation drift
Cross-bug interactions create degradation feedback loops: Bug 2+5 (trigger dead), Bug 1+4 (bloat loop), Bug 3+6 (code+reasoning lost), Bug 4+5 (accelerating degradation)

Triple design contradiction: code is precious (×1.3) but not precious enough (0.5 floor < 0.7 gate) but should be destroyed (50-char stub). Structured content blind spot: type=code blocks invisible to protection.

open_questions.question: What is the actual runtime performance of estimateTokens on large message arrays?
Should the extension use tree-sitter or similar AST parsing for code block understanding?
options: Use LLM for semantic code understanding
